title: select result is mismatch when use COALESCE and not.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3 (c1 INT NOT NULL, c2 VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL, c3 DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO t3 (c1, c2, c3) VALUES (1, 'test', 10.50), (2, NULL, 20.75), (3, 'example', NULL);
SELECT COALESCE(c2, 'default') AS result FROM t3 WHERE c1 = 2;
SELECT c1 FROM t3 WHERE c3 IS NULL;
SELECT c1 FROM t3 WHERE COALESCE(c2, 'fallback') = 'example';
key_bug_features: COALESCE function returns unexpected results in SQL query
